reasoning: The baseline 10x spike requirement sets an extremely high bar for a positive resolution. India normally sustains dozens to hundreds of politically related fatalities per year according to ACLED data. To spike to 10 times the monthly average would require an unprecedented outbreak of mass casualties or civil war, for which there are no current indicators. Alternatively, the "1.000" baseline mentioned in the prompt strongly suggests a data glitch or temporary cessation of data collection for India (meaning an average of 0 fatalities). If this glitch is retroactive, it will either be fully backfilled (returning the baseline to normal levels and making a 10x spike unlikely) or it will persist into the forecasting window (resulting in 0 fatalities for the resolution period, making it impossible to breach 10). Given the short forecast horizons (ranging from 1 week to 3 months) and the structural rarity of massive 10x Black Swan fatality spikes, the probabilities are kept exceptionally low, anchored to the minimum allowed bound and slowly increasing to reflect general timeline uncertainty.
